claim_boundary: Founder objective and profile-selection law, not a claim that the current runtime or any current interface satisfies it.

objectives.description: Completes unfamiliar end-to-end objectives with correct terminal artifacts.
Recovers from verified failure without false completion or authority bypass.
Bounds claims of completion when the requested end state does not exist.
Lets the user invoke, direct, interrupt, inspect, and resume work through the profile's declared modalities.
Preserves authorized work across the profile's declared foreground, background, suspension, and handoff boundaries.
Measures end-to-end interaction latency for the declared profile on the exact environment.
Preserves observation, notification, tool, Maker-effect, and user-consent authority without exceptions.
Keeps data and execution within the profile's declared local, hybrid, or remote privacy boundary.
Compares sustained energy cost without making low power more important than critical capability gates.
